reporters.name: Maya Okafor

pitchReadiness.note: Strongest fit on the deck, but she'll ask for a utility partner on record — background only today.

reporters.theOpen: Most grid-storage stories chase new batteries. This one starts with forgotten dams.
reporters.whyTheyCare: Has written three pieces this year on the gap between storage targets and deployed capacity. Ferrowatt's dispatch data directly answers a question she raised in her May feature: where does 'boring' storage capacity actually come from?

recentCoverage.title: The storage build-out is behind schedule. Nobody wants to say it.
recentCoverage.note: Argued the industry over-indexes on new-build lithium projects.

recentCoverage.title: Why grid operators are quietly re-rating old hydro
recentCoverage.note: Covered the exact asset class Ferrowatt retrofits.

proofMatchNotes: 18 months of dispatch logs match her preference for operational data over projections
Pacific Northwest sites are in her home coverage region
cautions: Skeptical of funding-led pitches — lead with the dispatch data, not the round
Will ask for utility partners on record; only background is available
reporters.suggestedAngle: The cheapest grid battery is a dam built in 1962
reporters.preferredFormat: Short email with data summary up top; no attachments

reporters.name: Daniel Reyes

pitchReadiness.note: Site visits and local interviews are on offer — everything his format needs is available now.

reporters.theOpen: Fourteen dams your readers drive past every week just started earning money on the wholesale power market.
reporters.whyTheyCare: Covers what infrastructure money means for towns in the region. Fourteen retrofit sites in Oregon and Washington is a local-jobs-and-land-use story before it is a climate story.

recentCoverage.title: Small towns, big transformers: the substation land rush
recentCoverage.note: Strong reader response on rural infrastructure economics.

recentCoverage.title: Who profits when the grid comes to the county line?
recentCoverage.note: Frames infrastructure through local ownership and tax base.

proofMatchNotes: Named retrofit sites give him concrete places and people to visit
CEO's dam-operator background fits his practitioner-profile style
cautions: Less interested in the funding round itself — frame around site communities
Needs site access or local interviews; pitch will stall without them
reporters.suggestedAngle: Fourteen forgotten dams in the Northwest just became grid assets
reporters.preferredFormat: Email or phone; open to site visits with notice

reporters.name: Priya Natarajan

pitchReadiness.note: Hold until the cost claim is independently verified — she fact-checks pitches in public.

reporters.theOpen: You wrote that climate tech has an announcement problem. Here's a Series A with 18 months of dispatch logs attached.
reporters.whyTheyCare: Tracks which climate verticals are getting funded versus deployed. A Series A with 18 months of revenue-generating operation cuts against the 'all announcements, no electrons' critique she has been making.

recentCoverage.title: Climate tech's announcement problem
recentCoverage.note: Publicly tired of funding rounds pitched as news.

recentCoverage.title: The deployment issue: 11 companies actually shipping
recentCoverage.note: Annual list; Ferrowatt's profile fits the inclusion criteria.

proofMatchNotes: Live deployment record fits her 'actually shipping' filter
Investor available for comment matches her funding-analysis format
cautions: Has publicly mocked dollar-amount-first pitches — never lead with the $12M
High inbound volume; receptivity is the weakest score on this card
Newsletter format means a mention, not a feature, is the realistic outcome
reporters.suggestedAngle: A Series A measured in megawatt-hours, not promises
reporters.preferredFormat: Two-paragraph email, plain text, no follow-up before 5 business days
